channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Street lighting doesn’t work, 6th March
item.description:
Lamppost doesn’t work and hasn’t for a while. The floor is also uneven so creates a huge fall hazard
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St John Fisher Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned vehicle, 6th March
item.description:
White Mazda car has been parked opposite our house since 22nd February. Not been moved since. There is a cone behind the car.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crossway, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown bush, 6th March
item.description:
Bush is restricting access to the foot path and is almost completely covering the pedestrian area
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Welland Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lighting is not on, 6th March
item.description:
Lighting is out leading up to dutton street from blissett street. Pitch black and dangerous especially when you have groups of youth hanging about in stairwell and ramps .
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Dutton Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter has been here for weeks, 6th March
item.description:
Litter has been here for weeks And is becoming a hazard.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Yoakley Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Communal bins were not emptied for the flats at Morvale Close, Belvedere, 6th March
item.description:
Two weeks ago someone cleared all the dumped rubbish that was near the communal bins and threw it all into the bins instead of taking it away meaning the tenants no longer had anywhere to throw their rubbish. The tenants have balanced bin bags on top of the bins and they’ve since fallen off the top and split everywhere. There’s food and rubbish all over the floor. There’s loads of flies etc. The bins were meant to be taken away on Monday, but they couldn’t be emptied so now the situation has got worse. People are now putting their bags on the floor next to the bins as there is no room for their bags. The rubbish is spilling out onto the pathway that tenants have to walk across to get to the front door. Also the people who cleared the rubbish near the bins initially have wedged too much into the cardboard bins, so they weren’t collected either.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Upper Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Kerb stone dislodged, 6th March
item.description:
Kerb stone dislodged by vehicles mounting pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Royal Parade, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cars jumping red lights, 6th March
item.description:
I live at Sandringham flats and so use the pedestrian crossing at the Cambridge Circus junction almost daily. Every day when I wait to cross the road, cars that have exited one part of the junction regularly jump red lights to avoid being stationary in the middle of the junction. This means that they are regularly moving through the pedestrian crossings whilst there is a green man, often meaning pedestrians are unable to cross there as the stream of traffic never stops before the red man is displayed again. it’s very unsafe and pedestrian unfriendly, and needs attention ASAP.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Charing Cross Road, Covent Garden, City of Westminster
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lighting at Kennington Open Space, 6th March
item.description:
None of the lighting from Bolton Crescent to Hillington Street and then around the play park is working. I'd suggest that it is quite unsafe for people coming home from work.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hillingdon Street, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bad surface Hansol road., 6th March
item.description:
Amazingly have been told problem with the road surface has been fixed!..Nothing at all has been done so why say dealt with, simply NOT TRUE , please investigate this response
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hansol Road, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish across street, 6th March
item.description:
A lot of domestic rubbish in this area, spreading across the street.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Norcott Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Abandoned garden bin, 6th March

item.title: Lighting at Kennington Open Space, 6th March
item.description:
None of the lighting from Bolton Crescent to Hillington Street and then around the play park is working. I’d suggest that it is quite unsafe for people coming home from work.

item.title: Abdoned childs bike, 6th March
item.description:
A children's bike has been left in the play park many days now. It is broken and needs removing.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Footpath From Crossmead To Broad Lawn, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Traffic cones need removing, 6th March
item.description:
The pavement was fixed last week and the four traffic cones need removing.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Footpath From Crossmead To Broad Lawn, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish and large items not being removed, 6th March
item.description:
This picture is from AFTER the bin men came and emptied the big bin this morning. There are cardboard boxes that have been there over a week, large items that haven’t been collected, bags full of what looks like building materials I haven’t looked inside and bags of household waste. Maybe your employees should start doing their jobs properly or be held accountable if they’re not. I speak on behalf of myself and my neighbours when I say that is NOT what we want to be looking at when we look out of our windows, or climbing over to get to vehicles. It’s disgusting and clearly nobody is responsible enough to organise getting it cleared away. What to the men who are supposed to keep the close clean do during the day? They’re always outside speaking loud on their phones but they haven’t used their common sense to contact the relevant department to sort out this issue if it isn’t their specific role?!? Let’s be serious.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wivenhoe Close, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light on road is out, 6th March
item.description:
Street light on road is out, not coming on at night.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Warwick Grove, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light out at night, 6th March
item.description:
Street light for the car park is broken, not coming on at night.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Warwick Grove, Hackney, London

item.title: Deep pothole, 6th March
item.description:
Outside number 189 Farnaby Road. There are a few along that stretch towards the golf club.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Farnaby Road, Shortlands, Greater London
